nodes.name: HRPPipelineRun
nodes.description: Executes the full Hierarchical Risk Parity pipeline: ingests asset return data, constructs a hierarchical clustering structure via a correlation/distance matrix, applies recursive bisection to allocate risk, and emits final portfolio weights.

inputs.name: data
inputs.type_desc: array-like | DataFrame of shape (T, N) — T observations across N assets (returns or prices)
inputs.constraints: No NaN values; N >= 2; T > N recommended for stable covariance estimation

outputs.name: weights
outputs.type_desc: array-like | Series of shape (N,) — normalised portfolio weights summing to 1.0

nodes.type_signature: (data: array-like | DataFrame of shape (T, N) — T observations across N assets (returns or prices)) -> array-like | Series of shape (N,) — normalised portfolio weights summing to 1.0
nodes.conceptual_summary: HierarchicalRecursiveBisectionWeightAllocator. Transforms a (T × N) matrix of co-varying scalar time-series into a dendrogram by converting pairwise correlation into a metric distance and applying agglomerative hierarchical clustering. The resulting binary tree is then traversed via recursive bisection, at each split proportionally assigning a risk budget (inverse-variance) to the two sub-clusters, until leaf-level allocations are resolved. The output is a probability simplex vector of N non-negative weights summing to unity, where each weight encodes the fraction of total risk budget assigned to that leaf node.. Applications: Computational neuroscience — allocating signal reconstruction budget across N neural recording channels whose firing-rate time-series exhibit hierarchically clustered co-activation patterns, ensuring that correlated neuron ensembles are not over-represented in a downstream decoder, Wireless sensor network load balancing — distributing transmission power or sampling duty cycles across N sensors whose measurement sequences form correlation clusters, using recursive bisection to equalize the variance contribution of each sensor cluster to a fused global estimate, Genomics / multi-omics integration — assigning relative weights to N genomic features (gene-expression probes, methylation sites, etc.) measured across T biological samples, where hierarchical clustering of the feature correlation matrix reveals co-regulated modules that should share rather than dominate the aggregate explanatory budget, Robotic swarm task allocation — partitioning a collective action budget (energy, time, bandwidth) among N agents whose behavioral time-series are correlated due to shared environmental stimuli, using the dendrogram to identify functionally redundant sub-groups and recursively rebalance effort so no correlated cluster monopolizes resources
